kill_fact: The catalyst is GENUINELY LIVE and the load-bearing facts survive primary-source verification: signed merger ($155.00 cash + 0.7720 CTAS), shareholder vote PASSED 06-12-2026 (>99% of votes cast, ~95% of shares) per UNF 8-K, and the ONLY open gate (FTC antitrust) is real and unresolved — the Second Request was issued 06-11-2026 to BOTH parties, extending the HSR period until 30 days after substantial compliance (CTAS 8-K). The $350M reverse-termination fee and Jan-10-2027 outside date (+up to 8mo extensions) are confirmed in the merger agreement. BUT the asymmetry is even-money at best, NOT the 3.79:1 the Catalyst-Watch board originally carried (its $257.91 floor was a near-current-price artifact; the dossier itself already corrected this). Live UNF ~$261-276 (mixed June reads: $272.60 on 06-10, $261 on 06-19) vs a live deal value ~$286-288 (CTAS ~$169-171) = gross upside only ~+4% to +10%; a hard-block floor ~$215-222 (standalone $200-225 traded $180-190 pre-deal + ~$18-19/sh break fee) is ~-16% to -20%. Roughly 0.5:1 raw — the wide spread is the market pricing real #1-buys-#3 antitrust risk, not free carry. The floor is NOT a hard contractual backstop: ~46% of consideration is CTAS stock, already down ~23%, eroding deal value independent of close.
corrections: Downside floor is ~$215-222 (standalone $200-225 + $18-19/sh reverse break fee), NOT the board's stale $257.91; corrected risk/reward is even-money (~0.5:1), NOT 3.79:1 — the candidate dossier already made this correction itself and is not relying on the stale floor. Live UNF price is mixed in the data ($272.60 on 06-10 vs $261 on 06-19), so upside ranges ~+4% (at $276) to ~+10% (at $261); either way the carry is thin and probability-weighted EV sits at or near the live price. Nov-30-2026 clearance is an estimate; only firm dates are the Jan-10-2027 outside date + extensions. The FTC Second Request on a #1-buys-#3 horizontal is a genuine block/divestiture risk the bull may under-weight.
evidence: UNF 8-K / press release (06-12-2026): UniFirst shareholders approved the Cintas merger, >99% of votes cast in favor (~95% of all common + Class B shares); terms $155.00 cash + 0.7720 CTAS/share; close expected H2 calendar 2026 — sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0000717954/000114036126025000/ef20075767_ex99-1.htm

CTAS 8-K (filed re 06-11-2026): 'On June 11, 2026, each of Cintas and UniFirst received a request for additional information (the Second Request)' from the FTC; HSR waiting period extends until 30 days after both parties substantially comply; Cintas continues to expect close in H2 calendar 2026 — sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0000723254/000095010326008890/dp248355_8k.htm

Merger agreement / DEFM14A: $350M reverse termination fee payable by Cintas on antitrust failure; UniFirst $213.3M company termination fee; outside/End Date Jan-10-2027 with automatic extensions up to two 4-month periods — sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0000717954/000114036126020380/ny20072400x1_defm14a.htm

Live quotes (06-2026): UNF ~$275-276, CTAS ~$169-170 → live deal value ~$155 + 0.7720x170 = ~$286; gross spread to $276 is only ~$11/+4%, sharply narrower than the dossier's ~$24/8.5% — stockanalysis.com/stocks/unf/
